1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→###########################################################################
     3→# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
     4→# built-in libraries.
     5→#
     6→# Input:
     7→#  $1: CPU name
     8→#      supported CPU list:
     9→#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
    10→#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
    11→#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
    12→#                            nx27v_purec
    13→#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
    14→#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
    15→#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
    16→#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
    17→#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
    18→#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
    19→#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
    20→#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
    21→#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
    22→#                            ax66_purec
    23→#  $2: compiler name
    24→#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
    25→#      are:
    26→#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
    27→#        - riscv32-elf-clang
    28→#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
    29→#        - riscv64-elf-clang
    30→#  $3: the target to run the executables
    31→#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
    32→#
    33→# Example:
    34→#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
    35→#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    36→#
    37→#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
    38→#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    39→#
    40→#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
    41→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    42→#
    43→#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
    44→#  and clang compiler
    45→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    46→###########################################################################
    47→
    48→CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
    49→COMPILER_NAME="$2"
    50→if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    51→    TARGET="BOARD"
    52→else
    53→    TARGET="${3}"
    54→fi
    55→
    56→function log () {
    57→    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    58→}
    59→
    60→##### misc settings #####
    61→LIB_NAME="libnn"
    62→LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
    63→TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
    64→ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
    65→
    66→# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
    67→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    68→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
    69→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    70→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    71→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    72→    CPU_FLAGS=""
    73→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
    74→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    75→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
    76→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    77→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    78→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    79→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
    80→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    81→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    82→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    83→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    84→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    85→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    86→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    87→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    88→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    89→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    90→    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    91→    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
    92→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    93→    else
    94→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    95→    fi
    96→
    97→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    98→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    99→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   100→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
   101→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   102→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
   103→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   104→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
   105→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
   106→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
   107→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   108→else
   109→    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
   110→    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
   111→- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
   112→- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
   113→- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
   114→- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
   115→- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"
   116→
   117→    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   118→    exit 1
   119→fi
   120→
   121→# check whether the compiler name is valid
   122→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   123→   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
   124→    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
   125→        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
   126→                "The available compilers are:\n" \
   127→                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
   128→                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
   129→        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   130→        exit 1
   131→    fi
   132→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="$2"
if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

content.text:
The file only has **44 lines** and contains just **activation** and **basic** groups. It looks like the perf run was **interrupted or still in progress** — it didn't finish running through all the function categories (convolution, fully connected, pooling, softmax, etc.).

The test is driven by `test_perf.sh` which calls `test.sh` with `-DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS` and runs on BOARD (FPGA). The run likely stopped partway through — either the board connection dropped, it timed out, or was manually cancelled. The `basic` group at the end is also incomplete (only 8 entries).

You'd need to re-run `test_perf.sh` to get the full dataset across all groups (convolution, fully connected, pooling, softmax, concatenation, etc.).

message.content: show me the cmd to run test_perf.sh

content.text:
Based on the script I read earlier, here's the usage:

```bash
./test_perf.sh <CPU_NAME> <COMPILER> <TARGET>
```

For your AX45MPV FPGA setup:

```bash
./test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD
```

It runs from a build directory, e.g.:

```bash
mkdir build_perf && cd build_perf
../test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD
```

The script calls `test.sh` with `-DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS` flags and outputs results into a `test_sh_<TIMESTAMP>/` folder containing `performance.csv` and `parameter.csv`.

The existing baseline at `build_perf_baseline/test_sh_20260321162450/` now has all groups (activation, basic, concatenation, convolution, fully_connected, pooling, softmax, util) — 289 entries total.

Looking at the script — `BOARD` is just passed through as the `TARGET` argument to `test.sh`, which tells it to run test binaries on the **FPGA board** (via the board-specific sim wrapper like `project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board`).

As opposed to `test.sh`'s other targets like `GDB` (GDB simulator) or `SID` (Andes SID simulator), `BOARD` means the test executables are uploaded and run on the physical FPGA hardware — which is what gives you real cycle counts for the performance CSV.

In your case that's the AX45MPV FPGA board at `sw-boards.andestech.com:1116`.

content.text:
It ran — but **every test failed**. The `accuracy_fail_list` has 257 failures, and the `performance.csv` only has `x,x` entries (all f16/s4 tests that can't run on the board).

The 3-second runtime means it couldn't actually execute any binaries on the FPGA board. The board wrapper `project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` likely couldn't connect to the FPGA at `sw-boards.andestech.com:1116` — either the board is offline, the SSH/telnet connection timed out, or the board is locked by another user.

You can verify by trying to connect to the board directly, or check the individual test logs:

```bash
# Check a specific test log for the actual error
cat test_sh_20260323054018/log/t_activate_s8.log | tail -20
```

content.text:
The compiler doesn't recognize `-mvh` and `-mext-vector`. Your `riscv64-elf-gcc` in PATH is not the **Andes toolchain** — it's likely a standard RISC-V GCC that doesn't support Andes-specific flags.

You need to use the Andes `ast530` toolchain. Check if it's available:

```bash
# Try the Andes toolchain path
which nds64le-elf-gcc
# or check the typical install location
ls /opt/andes/*/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc
```

The `test_perf.sh` needs to run with the Andes-patched `riscv64-elf-gcc` that supports `-mext-vector`, `-mvh`, etc. The `-mvh` flag comes from `test.sh` (not `test_perf.sh`), which adds board-specific flags for the FPGA target.

file.content:
---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="$2"
if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="$2"
if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

content.text: Now also clear the `First_Reset_done` flag and default the CPU to `ax45mpv`:

input.new_string:
CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

lines: CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
-# Clear board reset flag so first test reinitializes the FPGA

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

content.text:
Done. Now you can just run:

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
../test_perf.sh
```

No arguments needed — defaults to `ax45mpv`, `riscv64-elf-gcc`, `BOARD`. The script will:
1. Prepend ast530 toolchain to PATH
2. Set `MYPC`/`MYPORT` for the FPGA board
3. Clear `First_Reset_done`
4. Run all perf tests

You can still override anything: `../test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-clang BOARD`

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

    46→###########################################################################

   100→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
