   109→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
   110→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   111→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
   112→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   113→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
   114→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   115→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
   116→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
   117→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
   118→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   119→else
   120→    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
   121→    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
   122→- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
   123→- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
   124→- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
   125→- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
   126→- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"
   127→
   128→    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   129→    exit 1
   130→fi
   131→
   132→# check whether the compiler name is valid
   133→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   134→   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
   135→    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
   136→        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
   137→                "The available compilers are:\n" \
   138→                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
   139→                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
   140→        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   141→        exit 1
   142→    fi
   143→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
   144→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
   145→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
   146→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
   147→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
   148→    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
   149→        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
   150→                "The available compilers are:\n" \
   151→                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
   152→                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
   153→        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   154→        exit 1
   155→    fi
   156→else
   157→    # here will not be reached
   158→    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
   159→fi
   160→
   161→# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
   162→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
   163→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
   164→fi
   165→
   166→# select the library to be linkd
   167→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
   168→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
   169→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
   170→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
   171→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
   172→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
   173→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
   174→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
   175→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
   176→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
   177→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
   178→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
   179→else
   180→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
   181→fi
   182→
   183→# select the running on board wrapper
   184→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   185→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   186→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   187→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   188→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
   189→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   190→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   191→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   192→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   193→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   194→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   195→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   196→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   197→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   198→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   199→else
   200→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   201→fi
   202→
   203→# -----
   204→# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
   205→# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
   206→# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
   207→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
   208→# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
   209→# cores.
   210→# -----
   211→# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
   212→${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   213→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   214→    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
   215→    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
   216→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
   217→    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   218→        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
   219→        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
   220→            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
   221→        fi
   222→    fi
   223→fi
   224→
   225→##### main ######
   226→rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
   227→log "***************************************************************************************"
   228→log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
   229→log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
   230→log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
   231→log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
   232→log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
   233→log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
   234→log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
   235→log "***************************************************************************************"
   236→
   237→EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
   238→${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
   239→
   240→log "***************************************************************************************"
   241→log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
   242→log "***************************************************************************************"
   243→
   244→# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
   245→out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
   246→mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
   247→

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

## Testing

```bash
# Run tests from a separate build directory:
mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir

# Plain C algorithm with GDB simulator
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3

# DSP algorithm with RV32 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

# Vector extension (RVV 1.0) with RV64 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"

# DAILY mode (includes code coverage via gcov)
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <target> DAILY [flags]
```

Unit tests are in `Examples/unit_func/t_*.c` (~301 test files). Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/`.

### QEMU Validation (preferred for Claude Code)

**IMPORTANT:** In Claude Code's sandbox, Andes QEMU's semihosting output goes to fd 9 instead of stdout. The standard `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` and QEMU wrapper scripts will produce zero output and report 0% pass rate. Always use the `qemu-validate/` scripts with `--strace` mode instead.

```bash
# Build libnn
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

libnn is the Andes Neural Network Library — a C library of optimized NN operators for RISC-V and NDS32 architectures. It provides multiple implementation variants per function: plain C (portable reference), DSP-optimized (RISC-V P-extension), and Vector-optimized (RVV 0.8 and 1.0). It maintains a CMSIS-NN compatibility layer for ARM portability.

## Optimization Focus

Primary target: **AX45MPV FPGA board** with **VLEN=1024**. FPGA cycle counts are the ground truth for performance. QEMU is for correctness verification only. When optimizing, always validate at VLEN=1024 and consider VLEN=512/256 for portability but do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 performance for smaller VLENs.

## Build Commands

```bash
# Build the library for a specific CPU target
./build.sh <CPU_NAME> [EXTRA_FLAGS] [COMPILER] [OUTPUT_PATH]

# Examples:
./build.sh D25                          # RV32 with DSP
./build.sh NX27V                        # RV64 with vector extension
./build.sh AX45MPV                      # RV64 with vector, 45-series tuning
./build.sh x64                          # Native x86-64 build (for host testing)

# Supported CPUs: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27,
#   N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV, AX46MPV, x86, x64
```

Output: `libnn.a` static library + `libnn.objdump` disassembly.

## Testing

```bash
# Run tests from a separate build directory:
mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir

# Plain C algorithm with GDB simulator
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3

# DSP algorithm with RV32 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

# Vector extension (RVV 1.0) with RV64 simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"

# DAILY mode (includes code coverage via gcov)
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <target> DAILY [flags]
```

Unit tests are in `Examples/unit_func/t_*.c` (~301 test files). Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/`.

### QEMU Validation (preferred for Claude Code)

**IMPORTANT:** In Claude Code's sandbox, Andes QEMU's semihosting output goes to fd 9 instead of stdout. The standard `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` and QEMU wrapper scripts will produce zero output and report 0% pass rate. Always use the `qemu-validate/` scripts with `--strace` mode instead.

```bash
# Build libnn
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh

# Run a single test (always use --strace in Claude Code)
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> --strace --vlen 1024

# Examples:
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
```

The `--strace` mode uses strace to capture semihosting writes and parses "accuracy checking pass/fail" from the trace. Tests can run in parallel for independent functions.

```bash
# Run the full test suite (16 parallel jobs, VLEN=1024)
/usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_all_tests.sh 16 1024
```

### Known Pre-existing Test Failures

The following 9 tests fail independent of any recent changes (verified at commit b552cecd and later):

- **`dup_s8_s16`, `dup_s8_s16_reordered`** — Compilation error: `test_malloc` implicit declaration (legacy test harness issue)
- **`mat_mul_kernel_q7`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_2`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_bias_2sft`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_q15_bias_2sft`, `mat_mul_kernel_q7_q15_reordered_bias_2sft`** — Same `test_malloc` compilation error
- **`lstm_unidirectional_s16`, `lstm_unidirectional_s8`** — Missing golden data (`"Error on reading golden!!!"`)
- **`get_version_libnn`** — Reports UNKNOWN (prints version string, no accuracy check)

## Architecture

### Source Organization

All source lives under `Source/` in 9 function categories:
- **ConvolutionFunctions/** — conv1x1, convHWC, depthwise conv, grouped conv, transposed conv
- **FullyConnectedFunctions/** — fully connected layers
- **ActivationFunctions/** — relu, sigmoid, tanh, silu, leaky relu, etc.
- **PoolingFunctions/** — average/max pooling
- **SoftmaxFunctions/** — softmax variants
- **BasicFunctions/** — elementwise add, multiply
- **ConcatenationFunctions/** — tensor concatenation
- **NNSupportFunctions/** — data conversion, requantization, buffer helpers
- **UtilFunctions/** — NMS, decode_dist2box, focal loss, etc.

### Function Naming Convention

```
riscv_nn_<operation>_<layout>_<input_type>_<weight_type>_<output_type>_<quant_mode>_<suffix>
```

Example: `riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any`
- `conv_1x1_HWC` — 1x1 convolution in HWC layout
- `s8_s8_s8` — signed 8-bit input, weight, output
- `asym` — asymmetric quantization (`sym` = symmetric, `sft` = shift-based)
- `bias` — includes bias addition
- `fast_any` — optimized variant, any channel count

Data types: `s4`, `s8`, `s16`, `f16`, `f32`, `u8`

### Multi-variant Implementation Pattern

Each function has up to 3 implementation paths selected at compile time via preprocessor guards:

1. **Vector (RVV)** — `#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA` with version checks via `NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION`
2. **DSP** — `#ifdef NDS_CPU_64` / P-extension intrinsics
3. **Plain C** — fallback, always present

Vector internal headers are versioned: `internal_vec_isa_v0_8.h`, `internal_vec_isa_v1_0.h`, etc.

### Headers

- **Public API** (`Include/`): `riscv_nn_convolution.h`, `riscv_nn_activation.h`, `riscv_nn_types.h`, etc.
- **Internal** (`internal/`): `internal_nn_math.h` (math macros/inline helpers), `internal_dsp_isa.h` (DSP intrinsics), `internal_vec_*.h` (vector intrinsics)

### CMSIS-NN Compatibility

`cmsis_include/` provides adapter headers (`convert_between_andes_nn_and_cmsis_nn.h`) mapping ARM CMSIS-NN APIs to libnn equivalents. Supports CMSIS-NN 3.0, 3.2, and 4.1.

## Commit Message Convention

Prefix with category tag in brackets:
- `[opt/rvv]` — RISC-V vector extension optimization
- `[opt/dsp]` — DSP extension optimization
- `[bug-fix]` — Bug fix
- `[new fun]` — New function added
- `[fine-tune]` — Algorithm tuning
- `[misc]` — Maintenance/cleanup

## Compilation Flags

Key defines that control code paths:
- `-DENA_VEC_ISA` — Enable vector ISA code paths
- `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80|1000` — Select RVV version (0.8 or 1.0)
- `-DMAX_VLEN=512|1024` — Maximum vector length
- `-DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` — Enable vector dot product instructions
- `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` — Auto-set for 45-series CPU tuning
- `-DENA_ACE_RVV` — Enable ACE (Andes Customization Engine) extensions

Base flags: `-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror`

lines: - **`lstm_unidirectional_s16`, `lstm_unidirectional_s8`** — Missing golden data (`"Error on reading golden!!!"`)
- **`get_version_libnn`** — Reports UNKNOWN (prints version string, no accuracy check)
+# Run from a build directory (defaults: ax45mpv, riscv64-elf-gcc, BOARD)

+The script auto-configures ast530 toolchain PATH, FPGA board env vars (`MYPC`, `MYPORT`), and clears `First_Reset_done`. Output: `test_sh_<TIMESTAMP>/performance.csv` with per-function instruction and cycle counts.

content.text:
There's no built-in filter for a single function. It always runs the full `test_list`. You'd need to do it manually — compile and run one test binary yourself:

```bash
# From build_perf_ax45mpv/, set up env:
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116

# Compile a single perf test (e.g. avepool_HWC_s8):
riscv64-elf-gcc -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections \
  -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
  -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1 -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS \
  -DGETENV_TESTDIR=/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func \
  -I/usr/riscv64-elf/include -I/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/include \
  -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
  -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mzfh \
  -o t_avepool_HWC_s8.adx \
  /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/t_avepool_HWC_s8.c -lnn_v -lm

# Run on board:
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board t_avepool_HWC_s8.adx
```

file.content:
##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

# skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # dump the disassembly
        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"

        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"

        # check correctness
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
            # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
            # code even if it enounters some issues.)
            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
            # the test program is executed.
            grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log_file} &> /dev/null
            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
                accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
                echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log_file}
            else
                accuracy_pass_num=$((accuracy_pass_num + 1))

                #dump inst/cycle into csv
                echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'inst' ${log_file}  | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'cycle' ${log_file} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                echo >> ${PERF_CSV}  #new line
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                # following parser will work properly only as below assumptions
                # are valid
                #   (1) each parameter is seperated with semicolon except the
                #       last one
                #   (2) the parameters are surrounded by the "param_hint_begin"
                #       and "param_hint_end"

                #dump the parameters into csv
                local param_hint_begin="___PARAMETERS_BEGIN___"
                local param_hint_end="___PARAMETERS_END___"
                local tmp_param_str=$(sed -n "/${param_hint_begin}/,/${param_hint_end}/p" ${log_file})

                # remove all newlines
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\r'/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\n'/}
                # remove hint string
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_begin}/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_end}/,}
                # make each parameter being in one line
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//;/;$'\n'}
                echo -e "$2,$1,${tmp_param_str}" >> ${PARAM_CSV}
            fi
        else
            accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
        fi
    else
        # build the executable fail
        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi

    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
        calculate_code_size "${1}"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# build and run the target configuration diagnosis program
#  $1: source file name of the program

#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

#note. the code coverage testing is not supported on board
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "DAILY" ] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(SID|QEMU)$ ]] && [[ "${CC}" != *"clang" ]] && [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 0 ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} --coverage -DGCOV"
    CODE_COVERAGE="1"
else
    CODE_COVERAGE="0"
fi

DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -Wl,-gc-sections"
if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    # only enable the performance counter for elf toolcahins
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS+=" -DPF_COUNTER"

    # enable virtual hosting / semihosting
    if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(BOARD|QEMU|ALIOTH)$ ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mvh"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == "BOARD" ]] && [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_QILAI_LD"* ]]; then
        # applying the customized linker script for Qilai platform
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V3)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d10/nds32.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "ENA_LM" ]] ; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V5)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d25/ae350-xip.ld"
    fi
fi

# app and lib cflags for v-ext
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FS_VS"
    fi

    # As using v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
        # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    fi
fi

# extra cflags for the environment with HVM
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_HVM"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
fi

# setup the extra flags for the glibc toolchain
if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS+=" -fpic"
fi

# customized crt0 for v-ext 0.8
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -nostartfiles ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx25v/crt0.s"
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#  $4: (optional) single function name to test
#      When specified, only this function is compiled and run.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-5: measure a single function with defaults (ax45mpv)
#  ./test_perf.sh "" "" "" avepool_HWC_s8
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

input.new_string:
##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
    log "SINGLE_FUNC: ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
fi
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"

if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
    #####################################################################
    # Single-function mode: compile and run one test directly
    #####################################################################
    UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
    SRC_FILE="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.c"
    if [[ ! -f "${SRC_FILE}" ]]; then
        echo "Error: test source not found: ${SRC_FILE}"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Build flags matching what test.sh uses for BOARD + v-ext perf tests
    APP_CFLAGS="-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    INC_DIR="-I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
    ADX_FILE="t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.adx"

    build_cmd="${COMPILER_NAME} ${APP_CFLAGS} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_FILE} ${SRC_FILE} ${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS -lm"
    log "Build: ${build_cmd}"
    ${build_cmd} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "Error: compilation failed for ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Running ${SINGLE_FUNC} on board..."
    log "Start at $(date)"
    ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_FILE} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    log "End at $(date)"

    # Parse inst/cycle from output
    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
        inst=$(grep -w 'inst' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
        cycle=$(grep -w 'cycle' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
        log ""
        log "Result: ${SINGLE_FUNC}  inst=${inst}  cycle=${cycle}"
    else
        log "Error: board execution failed (ret=${ret})"
    fi
else
    #####################################################################
    # Full test suite mode
    #####################################################################
    ${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
fi

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log into test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder (full mode only)
if [[ -z "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
    out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
    mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#  $4: (optional) single function name to test
#      When specified, only this function is compiled and run.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-5: measure a single function with defaults (ax45mpv)
#  ./test_perf.sh "" "" "" avepool_HWC_s8
###########################################################################

##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
fi
export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"

CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"

if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

SINGLE_FUNC="$4"

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

lines: +    # Single-function mode: compile and run one test directly
+    # Build flags matching what test.sh uses for BOARD + v-ext perf tests
-# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
+# move the log into test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder (full mode only)
